title: Tristan Sailor

{{Short description|Australian rugby league footballer}} {{use dmy dates |date=April 2020}} {{use Australian English |date= April 2020}} {{Infobox rugby league biography | name = Tristan Sailor | image = Tristan Sailor.jpg | position = {{rlp|FB|so|WG}} | birth_date = {{birth date and age|1998|06|23|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Brisbane, Queensland]] Australia | height = {{convert|1.79|m|ftin|abbr=on|order=flip}} | weight = {{convert|85|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} | club1 = {{nowrap|[[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]]}} | year1start = 2019 | year1end = 20 | appearances1 = 5 | tries1 = 1 | goals1 = 0 | fieldgoals1 = 0 | points1 = 4 | club2 = [[Brisbane Broncos]] | year2start = 2023 | year2end = 24 | appearances2 = 16 | tries2 = 1 | goals2 = 0 | fieldgoals2 = 0 | points2 = 4 | club3 = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] | year3start = 2025 | year3end = present | appearances3 = 48 | tries3 = 26 | goals3 = 16 | fieldgoals3 = 0 | points3 = 136 | new = yes | source = {{Cite web |title=Tristan Sailor - Career Stats & Summary |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/tristan-sailor/summary.html |website=Rugby League Project}} | updated = 11 June 2026 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = [[Wendell Sailor]] | mother = | spouse = | education = | relatives = [[Dane Gagai]] (cousin)
[[Jacob Gagai]] (cousin)
[[Josh Hoffman]] (cousin)
[[Travis Waddell]] (cousin) }} }} '''Tristan Sailor''' (born 23 June 1998) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} and {{rlp|so}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and [[St. George Illawarra]] in the NRL. ==Background== Sailor was born in [[Brisbane]] into a family of [[Torres Strait]] Islanders & [[Solomon Island]] descendants.{{Cite web |date=2020-07-30 |title=Indigenous Round v South Sydney |url=https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2020/07/30/nrl-team-indigenous-round-v-south-sydney/ |website=dragons.com.au}} His father, [[Wendell Sailor| a Solomon island descendant Wendell]], was a [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual international]] who played more than 300 combined professional games across the two codes of rugby.{{Cite web |date=2021-09-21 |title=Son also rises: Sailor jnr signs three-year Dragons deal |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/son-also-rises-sailor-jnr-signs-threeyear-dragons-deal-20130920-2u5b9.html |website=smh.com.au}} At the age of seven, Sailor moved to Sydney with his family before settling in [[Wollongong]] two years later and began playing junior rugby league for the [[Western Suburbs Red Devils]] in the [[Illawarra Rugby League]].{{Cite web |date=2016-09-02 |title=The Rock's mum gives a shout out to Illawarra rugby league |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/the-rocks-mum-gives-a-shout-out-to-illawarra-rugby-league-nrl-dwayne-johnson/1j4ik53wdc30h1bfl5vn81l07o |website=sportingnews.com.au}} Despite spending the majority of his upbringing in [[New South Wales]], Sailor's [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] eligibility for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] was confirmed in 2014 when the NRL introduced a father-son rule.{{Cite web |date=2016-06-13 |title=The eligibility rule delivering Queensland more NSW-bred players |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2016-the-eligibility-rule-delivering-queensland-more-nswbred-players-20160613-gphj07.html |website=smh.com.au}} Sailor confirmed later in 2014 that he had always wanted to represent Queensland.{{Cite web |date=2014-02-05 |title=Sailor may have been picked for the NSW Blues but his heart lies with Queensland |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/tristan-sailor-may-have-been-picked-for-the-nsw-blues-but-his-heart-lies-with-queensland/news-story/c294ceaa217aea5b4b1e9cfd2ddbcd63 |website=couriermail.com.au}} ==Career== ===St George Illawarra Dragons=== Sailor made his first grade debut in round 23 of the [[2019 NRL season]] for St George against the [[Sydney Roosters]] scoring a try in a 34-12 loss at [[Kogarah Oval]].{{Cite web |date=2019-08-24 |title=Round 23 NRL team announcements |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/20/round-23-nrl-team-announcements/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190824083103/https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/20/round-23-nrl-team-announcements/ |archive-date=2019-08-24 |website=NRL.com}}{{Cite web |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2019-08-25 |title=Sailor offers hope for the future after heavy loss to Roosters |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/24/sailor-gives-hope-for-the-future-after-heavy-loss-to-roosters/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190827075607/https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/08/24/sailor-gives-hope-for-the-future-after-heavy-loss-to-roosters/ |archive-date=2019-08-27 |website=NRL.com}} On 29 September 2019, Sailor was named in the [[2019 Canterbury Cup NSW]] team of the season.{{Cite web |date=2019-09-29 |title=2019 Canterbury Cup NSW Team of the Year |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/09/29/canterbury-cup-nsw-team-of-the-year-2019/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200417225742/https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/09/29/canterbury-cup-nsw-team-of-the-year-2019/ |archive-date=2020-04-17 |website=NSW Rugby League}} He was released by [[St. George Illawarra]] at the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].{{Cite news |last=Chisholm |first=Ed |date=2020-09-24 |title=St. George Illawarra Dragons prodigy Tristan Sailor told to look elsewhere for 2021 |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/dragons-inform-young-prodigy-to-look-elsewhere-as-griffin-begins-roster-overhaul/1gr3x96plywuq1mmsa8dd50tzg |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201022065235/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/dragons-inform-young-prodigy-to-look-elsewhere-as-griffin-begins-roster-overhaul/1gr3x96plywuq1mmsa8dd50tzg |archive-date=2020-10-22}} After being released by the club, Sailor's father [[Wendell Sailor]] spoke to the media and voiced his anger at his son being released, saying he was told by the club that if he had a big off-season, he would be in the mix this year but he never got any game time.”{{Cite news |last=Williamson |first=Nathan |date=2020-09-25 |title=Unwanted Dragon Tristan Sailor reportedly sounded out for shock rugby union move |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-news-tristan-sailor-storm-rugby-union-wendell-rugby-league/1axslnjyoxjyk11kc4rjcryc9p/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201022065623/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-news-tristan-sailor-storm-rugby-union-wendell-rugby-league/1axslnjyoxjyk11kc4rjcryc9p/ |archive-date=2020-10-22}} ===Souths Logan Magpies=== In April 2022, [[Souths Logan Magpies]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] competition announced they had signed Sailor for the rest of the season.{{Cite web |date=April 20, 2022 |title=Tristan Sailor to return via Queensland Cup after being found not guilty of sexual assault {{!}} Sporting News Australia |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/tristan-sailor-return-queensland-cup-after-being-found-not-guilty-sexual-assault/tbmlantdmrepiciqdlvgu8bs |website=www.sportingnews.com |language=en-au}} ===Brisbane Broncos=== On 16 December 2022, it was announced that Sailor had joined [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] on a train and trial contract ahead of the 2023 NRL season.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-full-list-of-trainandtrial-players-josh-reynolds-bulldogs-rabbitohs-broncos-ryley-smith-panthers-haizyn-mellars-nathaniel-roache-eels/news-story/1820b2b8844ca7dc899184475f8765d5/|title=From schoolboy sensations to journeymen eyeing last chance: Every NRL train-and-trial player|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} In round 13 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Sailor made his debut for the Brisbane Broncos against the New Zealand Warriors, playing at fullback. He had an impressive debut with the Brisbane outfit winning 26 - 22 without their [[State of Origin]] representative players. Sailor made a try saving tackle in the first half and set up the decisive try from a clever grubber kick in the second half. He played his second game for Brisbane against the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in round 18 which the Brisbane club won 24 - 16 at [[The Gabba]]. Sailor played a total of four matches with Brisbane in the 2023 NRL season but did not feature in their finals campaign nor grand final loss against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2023/oct/01/nrl-grand-final-2023-penrith-panthers-vs-brisbane-broncos-live-updates-scores-results-teams-squads-kick-off-time-accor-stadium-sydney/|title=NRL grand final 2023: Penrith Panthers defeat Brisbane Broncos – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}} In round 4 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Sailor made his first start of the year in the club's 38-12 victory over arch-rivals [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]]. On 4 April 2024, Sailor's father Wendell launched another attack on Sailor's former club St. George Illawarra saying “The way Tristan got treated at the Dragons was disgusting, Other Dragons kids have been let go … the culture of the club hasn’t been good since Wayne (Bennett) left, “Tristan loved the Dragons more than me, they picked Corey Norman and that’s fine, I understood that, but Tristan went from starting, to the bench, to 18th man, to No. 21 and 22 and out of the squad with no reason at all as to why. Then before a game against the Storm – their last game of the (2020) season – a Dragons official walks up to Tristan and says, ‘Thanks for your service, we no longer need you at this club, we’re going in a different direction’ Seriously, what a joke".{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/threw-him-out-wendell-sailor-slams-dragons-over-disgusting-treatment-of-son-tristan/news-story/0300d26350fb7093ef8f009f42e71477/|title=‘Threw him out’: Legend blasts Dragons for ‘disgusting’ treatment of son... and it ‘wasn’t just him’|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} ===St Helens=== On 8 August 2024, Sailor signed a two-year deal with English side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] starting in 2025.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12206/13192564/tristan-sailor-st-helens-sign-cross-code-great-wendell-sailors-son-from-brisbane-broncos-for-2025/|title=Tristan Sailor: St Helens sign cross-code great Wendell Sailor's son from Brisbane Broncos for 2025|website=www.skysports.com}} In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Sailor made his official club debut for St Helens and scored four tries in their 82-0 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cg5y9n6z024o|title=Sailor stars as 15-try St Helens thrash troubled Salford|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} Sailor played 29 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including their 20-12 semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 7 February 2026, Sailor scored a hat-trick for St Helens in their 98-2 victory over [[Workington Town]] in the third round of the Challenge Cup.{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/25834740.paul-rowley-said-st-helens-cup-win-workington/|title=What Paul Rowley said about St Helens' cup win at Workington|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}} ==Controversy== On 10 October 2020, Sailor was arrested and charged with aggravated sexual assault against a woman in a house in Sydney.{{Cite news |date=2020-10-10 |title=Jennings fails drugs test; Sailor arrested |work=BBC Sport |publisher= British Broadcasting Corporation |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54491097 |url-status=live |access-date=2020-10-10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201022070106/https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54491097 |archive-date=2020-10-22}} On 31 March 2022, Sailor was found not guilty on all charges.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/former-nrl-player-tristan-sailor-found-not-guilty-of-sexually-assaulting-woman/news-story/0947aba1e52fb04732bedec027f5a984?fbclid=IwAR30QbHfYj6l921z-w74XxSfcFvmXSBZgxwD6JQ8e42Mbbb5mx-9GRYM3yM/|title=Former NRL player Tristan Sailor found not guilty of sexually assaulting woman|website=www.foxsports.com.au}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links==
